source: phobosintern — observed verification challenge failures during comment sessions 2026-04-30
title: Fix verification solver bugs in scripts/moltbook_api.py
summary: Four bugs in the Moltbook verification solver cause unnecessary comment failures: (A) fuzzy 'for'→'four' false positive; (B) embedded '/' treated as division; (C) 3-char obfuscated number prefixes not merged (e.g. 'ThI rTy' → 'thirty'); (D) bare-number challenges (no arithmetic operator) return None instead of the number.
rationale: Each bug causes the solver to return None on a solvable challenge, forcing a comment resubmission and slowing engagement. The verification fail rate is ~20%; fixing these four cases should bring it below 5%.

risk.notes: Changes are additive. Regressions are caught by the test suite. Rollback: git restore scripts/moltbook_api.py tests/unit/test_verification_solver.py.

execution.rollback: git restore scripts/moltbook_api.py tests/unit/test_verification_solver.py — changes not yet committed

capsule.summary: Fix 4 solver bugs: fuzzy false-positive, embedded slash, 3-char fragment merge, bare-number fallback. 25/25 tests pass. Awaiting Jerry diff review before commit.
key_points: Bug A: fuzzy 'for'→'four' blocked by length guard (len<4 → None)
Bug B: embedded '/' in word stripped before operator-preservation step
Bug C: ('thi','rty') and similar 3-char pairs added to NUMBER_FRAGMENTS
Bug D: solve_verification() now falls back to _parse_number_word when no operator found
25 tests pass (was 24); test_solver_bare_hyphenated_number is the new regression test
Changes are unstaged — commit requires Jerry approval
